eager_512_16_decode_delta.interpretation: eager 512/16 minus 512/0 by kernel name; compact decode-family approximation used because full 512/128 graph rocprof did not produce a CSV.

notes: Graph 512/128 kernel trace was attempted first and did not produce a CSV in /tmp/p9_b7/rocprof-512-128-graph.log; retained 512/16 traces plus a 512/0 prefill-only trace to approximate decode deltas by kernel-name subtraction.

Pack8 selected GEMV kernels are present in decode deltas, but legacy prefill_out kernels remain a large bucket. Q6_K lm-head logits pack8 fallback is allowed by task text, but Q8_0 dense/shared prefill_out and any non-lm-head prefill_out decode buckets are not acceptable for a retained B7 row.
Eager decode is much slower wall-clock because launch overhead is not hidden; graph replay is still required for throughput once correctness and kernel mix are fixed.

blockers: P9.E2 correctness rejects the WMMA prefill + GEMV decode opt-in combination (KL/top-1 drift).
Decode median is ~63 tok/s, far below the 95 tok/s target and only +0.8% vs ~62.6 baseline.
Rocprof decode deltas show selected pack8 GEMV kernels are active, but legacy prefill_out kernels still consume a large bucket; decode needs GGUF repacking/layout work, not just graph replay.
